questions.text: How would you improve Google Maps for first-time visitors to a new city?
Design a product to help remote teams replace the value of unstructured hallway conversations.
You are the PM for Instagram Stories. A competitor launches a feature that drives 30 percent more engagement. How do you respond?
Imagine you are the CPO of Zoom facing competition from Teams. What would you do in the next 90 days?
How would you redesign the YouTube subscription experience for power users?
You have three features competing for the next quarter and you can only ship one. Walk me through how you decide.
Engineering says a feature you committed to will slip by six weeks. How do you handle the trade-off?
You have eight engineers and a roadmap that needs twelve. Where do you cut, and how do you communicate it?
Your top customer asks for a feature that 95 percent of users will never use. How do you respond?
Pick a product you use daily that you think is poorly designed. What would you change first, and why?
How would you measure the success of WhatsApp's voice messages feature?
How would you launch a new ride-sharing product in Mexico City?
Walk me through how you would define the north star metric for a B2B SaaS product.
You inherit a product with declining engagement. What is your first 30 days?
A feature you shipped is performing below expectations. How do you decide whether to iterate or kill it?
How would you decide which customer feedback to act on and which to ignore?
How would you decide between building a feature in-house or buying a vendor solution?
Explain how you would apply RICE to a backlog of fifteen ideas.
Walk me through how you balance bug fixes, technical debt, and new features in a quarterly plan.
A senior leader pushes a pet feature into your roadmap with no data. How do you handle it?
Tell me about a launch that failed. What did you take away from it?
Tell me about a time you influenced a team without formal authority.
Describe a situation where you had to say no to an executive. How did you handle it?
Tell me about a time you killed a feature because the data did not support it.
Describe a conflict between two engineering teams that you helped resolve.
What experiments would you run to test a new onboarding flow before committing engineering effort?
How do you structure a written PRD so that engineering and design can build from it without scheduling another meeting?
What is the difference between a leading and a lagging indicator? Give an example of each from a product you have shipped.
Walk me through how you would design an A/B test for a pricing change.
How do you keep your stakeholders informed without burying them in updates?
